mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC over 60-day horizon falls below 0.02 in any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts below 1,200 thousand units signals weakening demand expectations and reduced future construction activity, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the correlation between HOUST declines and subsequent negative returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment rate rises simultaneously, signaling supply-side weakness combined with labor market softening that reduces earnings power and equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.5% AND UNRATE increases > 0.2pp month-over-month
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure or supply shock environment
failure_condition: The joint signal (INDPRO deceleration AND UNRATE rise) fails to predict negative 5-day returns with Rank IC > 0.05, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) when tested against single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales and food services growth accelerates above 0.8% month-over-month while housing starts remain elevated above 1,350 thousand units, signaling broad-based consumer and residential demand strength that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.8% AND HOUST > 1,350 thousand units
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and residential sectors
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.04, or SPX produces negative returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rises above 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, creating a negative real interest rate regime that erodes fixed-income returns and redirects capital to equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: low real rates with above-target inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (negative real rate) does not significantly improve predictive power over single-series models (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate rises above 4.5% in a single month, signaling an abrupt deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers equity risk repricing and negative forward returns as investors anticipate earnings pressure from reduced consumer spending over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-stage recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC for this signal remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate while real retail sales simultaneously contract month-over-month, signaling a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0%
regime_hint: demand recession or growth stall environment
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.05, or SPX produces positive returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.
